        |Just in time, darling... I've been saving up some very warm, wet surprises for you. Come here!
    ]"

        |sit right onto the demon queen's lap, smiling as a pair of her wings curl comfortingly around your shoulders.

rows.original: "My story?" Kas echoes, pursing her lips. "Well, I do have a favorite story about a cow learning the dangers of magic..." "No," you sigh, "your story. Who really are you?" She tsks her tongue. "I know, dear. Are you going to deprive a girl of all her secrets just like that? Really now! But I suppose you have a right to know who you're dealing with after all this time. Fine, then. The truth of it." Your demonic lover sighs, leaning back into the cushions and curling her tail around your waist. "Let's seeeeee. I come from a plane called Mareth. It's... kind of a shithole now. There was a time when it was beautiful and abundant and so full of wonder... but the demons ruined all of that. Turned it into dumpster that the planes' trash falls into, never to escape. Before I was born, my ancestors used a powerful spell to transport their entire city from their home plane into Mareth. The story goes that they were explorers from a realm abundant and overflowing with magic, charting the cosmos in their enchanted city-ship. Mareth proved too much for them, though. The city crashed into the mountains on the realm's far eastern reaches, caused a great deal of damage to the arcane devices powering the place. It flew no more after that." "Around the time I was born, the gods of Mareth took notice of our presence on their virginal world, and started mutating the animals of the plane into beastkin like minotaurs and lupines and centaurs. Jealous of the human form, I suppose. Oh, and yes, I was human once, appearances aside. All of the city's inhabitants were magicians, though my father was what you'd call a spellblade — a knight who wove magic into his swordfighting. He was the Lord Commander of the Watch, the force our king erected to protect the city from the beastkin and monsters starting to flail cluelessly about beyond our walls. It might surprise you to learn that I followed in his footsteps, rather than those of my mother, an archmage." You glance at her giant axe sitting across the room. "Not really." Kas snorts. "I walked into that, didn't I? Alright, smart ass, let me tell you something else you already know while I'm at it: when I was mortal, my proper form of address was Ser Kasyrra Arellian, Lady Captain of the Watch. I was chosen to be a knight like my father when I was just a little girl. We were pressed on all sides by predatory beastkin just learning to strike fires from flint, while we commanded steel and arch-magic. It wasn't a contest to keep the city safe, but the mages were dreadfully afraid of the world they'd stumbled into all the same, especially the strange new gods within it. Worse, they couldn't leave. Mareth traps anything within it, refusing to allow anything to travel to planes beyond itself. The Watch's main role was protecting mage expeditions out into the wilderlands, down from the mountains into the deep forests and myriad lakes, even to the island palace of the goddess Marae, whence the name of the world itself. That was when I learned most of my black magic: accompanying my rancid bitch of a mother on countless pointless treks to understand the new world better, to try and find some new way of breaching the Veil of Worlds and returning their city whence it came. Mother never cared for me much, thought I was 'a dumb brute' but still, I suppose she considered it a point of pride that no child of hers should have been uneducated in the dark arts." You pause her. "So why did the mages come to Mareth in the first place?" "I don't know," Kas shrugs. "Not really. The murals in the Tower of the Griffon spoke of some terrible darkness unleashed on my ancestors' home world, of a great expedition set forth to chart the cosmos. Of a great and terrible source of magic unlike any they had ever encountered before, which they set out to find. Whether they were searching for a powerful weapon to defend their original world, or sought a new world to escape to, I couldn't tell you. Much of our history was lost in the transit to Mareth; it was a hard landing."
rows.translation: 我的故事？”卡丝轻声重复道，嘴唇微微撅起，“嗯，我确实有一个最喜欢的故事，讲的是一头牛如何明白了魔法的危险……” “不，”你叹了口气，你的 故事……你究竟是谁呢？” 她吐了吐舌头：“我知道的，亲爱的。你真的要就这样剥夺一个女孩的所有秘密吗？真的吗？不过我想，经过这么长时间，你确实有权利知道自己正在和谁打交道吧。 好吧，那就说吧。”你的恶魔恋人叹了口气，向后靠在靠垫上，把尾巴缠在你的腰上。“让我想想……我来自一个叫做玛瑞斯的星球。现在那里……已经变成了一片废墟。不过曾经，那里是非常美丽、富饶的，充满了各种神奇的事物。…但是那些恶魔毁了一切。他们把那里变成了一个垃圾场，飞机的废弃物都被扔进那里，永远无法逃脱。在我出生之前，我的祖先们使用了一个强大的咒语，将他们的整座城市从他们的母星转移到了玛瑞斯。 传说中，他们是来自一个充满魔力的神秘王国的探险者，在他们那艘被施了魔法的城市飞船中探索着宇宙。然而，玛瑞斯却给他们带来了巨大的麻烦——那座城市飞船坠毁在了该王国最东端的山脉中，导致为整个城市提供动力的那些神秘装置遭到了严重的破坏。 从那以后，它就再也没飞过了。” “大约在我出生的时候，马雷特的神明注意到了我们存在于他们这个原始的世界中，于是开始将这些大陆上的动物变异成半兽人形态，比如米诺陶洛斯、狼人以及半人马。 我想，我是嫉妒人类的外形吧。哦，没错，确实是这样。 人类 有一次，抛开外表不谈，这座城市里的所有居民都是魔法师。不过我的父亲属于那种将魔法融入剑术中的骑士——也就是你们所说的“法剑骑士”。他是守卫军的最高指挥官，而我们的国王正是组建这支军队，用来保护城市免受那些在城墙外四处肆虐的野兽和怪物的侵害。你可能会感到惊讶，但我选择追随他的脚步，而不是我那位大魔导师母亲的道路。 你瞥了一眼放在房间另一边的那把巨斧，说道：“其实并不是。” 卡斯哼了一声：“不就是我走进了那个地方吗？好吧，自作聪明的家伙，既然我正在这里，那就再告诉你一件你本来就已经知道的事情吧：在我还是凡人的时候，我的正式称呼应该是‘卡西拉·阿雷利安爵士’，也就是‘守卫队的女队长’。 在我还是个小女孩的时候，就被选中要像我的父亲一样成为一名骑士。当时，我们周围到处都是那些刚刚学会用打火石生火的掠食性兽人，而我们却掌握着钢铁武器与强大的弓箭魔法。这场斗争并不是为了保护城市的安宁，但法师们依然对这个世界充满了恐惧——尤其是那些突然出现在其中的陌生新神明。 更糟糕的是，他们根本无法离开。玛雷斯会将任何进入其范围内的东西都困住，绝不会让任何东西穿过它而前往外界。守望者组织的主要职责就是保护那些前往荒野地区的法师探险队——这些探险队会从山脉出发，穿过茂密的森林和众多的湖泊，甚至会前往玛拉女神的岛屿宫殿，而这个世界的名字也正是由此而来的。 就是在那个时候，我学会了大部分那些“黑魔法”：陪伴着我那个脾气暴躁、令人讨厌的母亲，参加了一次又一次毫无意义的远足，试图更好地了解这个新世界，寻找某种新的方法来突破“世界之幕”，让他们的城市重新回到原来的地方。 母亲从来都不太关心我，认为我是个“愚笨的家伙”，但即便如此，我想她应该也为自己的孩子们都没有在黑暗魔法方面受到教育而感到自豪吧。 你打断了她：“那么，那些法师最初为什么会来到马雷斯呢？” “我不知道，”卡斯耸了耸肩，“真的不知道。狮鹫塔里的壁画上记载着一些关于降临在我祖先故乡世界的可怕黑暗力量，还有关于一支为探索宇宙而出发的远征队的事情。 这是一种极其强大且可怕的魔法源泉，是他们迄今为止从未遇到过的。他们开始着手寻找这种魔法。究竟他们是想找到一件强大的武器来保卫自己的家园，还是想要寻找一个新的世界作为避难所，我实在无从知晓。 我们在前往马雷斯的途中失去了许多珍贵的历史资料；那真是一次艰难的旅程。
rows.raw: ' "My story?" Kas echoes, pursing her lips. "Well, I do have a favorite story about a cow learning the dangers of magic..." "No," you sigh, "your story. Who really are you?" She tsks her tongue. "I know, dear. Are you going to deprive a girl of all her secrets just like that? Really now! But I suppose you have a right to know who you're dealing with after all this time. Fine, then. The truth of it." Your demonic lover sighs, leaning back into the cushions and curling her tail around your waist. "Let's seeeeee. I come from a plane called Mareth. It's... kind of a shithole now. There was a time when it was beautiful and abundant and so full of wonder... but the demons ruined all of that. Turned it into dumpster that the planes' trash falls into, never to escape. Before I was born, my ancestors used a powerful spell to transport their entire city from their home plane into Mareth. The story goes that they were explorers from a realm abundant and overflowing with magic, charting the cosmos in their enchanted city-ship. Mareth proved too much for them, though. The city crashed into the mountains on the realm's far eastern reaches, caused a great deal of damage to the arcane devices powering the place. It flew no more after that." "Around the time I was born, the gods of Mareth took notice of our presence on their virginal world, and started mutating the animals of the plane into beastkin like minotaurs and lupines and centaurs. Jealous of the human form, I suppose. Oh, and yes, I was human once, appearances aside. All of the city's inhabitants were magicians, though my father was what you'd call a spellblade — a knight who wove magic into his swordfighting. He was the Lord Commander of the Watch, the force our king erected to protect the city from the beastkin and monsters starting to flail cluelessly about beyond our walls. It might surprise you to learn that I followed in his footsteps, rather than those of my mother, an archmage." You glance at her giant axe sitting across the room. "Not really." Kas snorts. "I walked into that, didn't I? Alright, smart ass, let me tell you something else you already know while I'm at it: when I was mortal, my proper form of address was Ser Kasyrra Arellian, Lady Captain of the Watch. I was chosen to be a knight like my father when I was just a little girl. We were pressed on all sides by predatory beastkin just learning to strike fires from flint, while we commanded steel and arch-magic. It wasn't a contest to keep the city safe, but the mages were dreadfully afraid of the world they'd stumbled into all the same, especially the strange new gods within it. Worse, they couldn't leave. Mareth traps anything within it, refusing to allow anything to travel to planes beyond itself. The Watch's main role was protecting mage expeditions out into the wilderlands, down from the mountains into the deep forests and myriad lakes, even to the island palace of the goddess Marae, whence the name of the world itself. That was when I learned most of my black magic: accompanying my rancid bitch of a mother on countless pointless treks to understand the new world better, to try and find some new way of breaching the Veil of Worlds and returning their city whence it came. Mother never cared for me much, thought I was 'a dumb brute' but still, I suppose she considered it a point of pride that no child of hers should have been uneducated in the dark arts." You pause her. "So why did the mages come to Mareth in the first place?" "I don't know," Kas shrugs. "Not really. The murals in the Tower of the Griffon spoke of some terrible darkness unleashed on my ancestors' home world, of a great expedition set forth to chart the cosmos. Of a great and terrible source of magic unlike any they had ever encountered before, which they set out to find. Whether they were searching for a powerful weapon to defend their original world, or sought a new world to escape to, I couldn't tell you. Much of our history was lost in the transit to Mareth; it was a hard landing." '

rows.original:
She continues after a pause for more wine. "So, I became a knight, a warrior-witch of the Watch. My training mixed martial and magical studies, much as a Savarran paladin's might. I spent a great deal of time exploring the world, protecting our wayfaring mages in their quests, sometimes even helping the local beastkin to learn what being human really meant. Ah, those sweet little cowgirls in the hills... can you tell, I have a certain affinity for them still?" she teases, cupping a milk-laden boob. "When it all fell apart — when our ancient mages finally overstepped for the last time — they were the ones I went to try and protect, not my own damned people. It was too late for them..."
        
        "What happened?"
        
        Kasyrra sips her wine again, golden eyes wandering over you for a long moment. "Something terrible. And wonderful. An apotheosis. They made themselves into demons! The ancient cabal, the oldest of our wizards, finally became so desperate that they turned to the forbidden soul arts to try and escape Mareth. They were so miserable in that beautiful, virginal world, so desperate to escape a paradise that they turned their own souls into fuel to try and power a spell strong enough to pierce the Veil of Worlds. One day the archmages all went into the Tower of the Dragon, proclaiming that they'd found the way to breach the Veil. By nightfall the city was overrun with demons, flooding from the tower like a tidal wave of corrupted cum and debauchery. I'd be shocked if fully half the lesser mages managed to escape. Almost none of the Watch did."
        
        "That was about twenty years ago. Our city's princess, Lethice — spoiled bitch of a bully that she was — became the queen of the demons sometime shortly after," Kas says with a sneer. "I never learned what happened to her father, the king. Probably bumped him off. I had been out on patrol when the first turning occured, and when I saw the corrupted flames burning and the winged figures flocking over the city walls, I turned and ran. Tried to rally the 'survivors' that I found and get them to safety. There were small churches of beastkin protected by holy wards that offered to take us in, but I myself made for the young cities of the west, where most of the non-human population was. I rallied fighters to come and help, to stand against the tide, while the very fabric of our world broke around us."
        
        Kas breathes out, long and weary. "And I failed. Our resistance came too little, too late, and washed away by a flood of demons. I was captured. Tortured. I fell."
        
        "Oh."
rows.translation:
她稍作停顿，又喝了一口酒，继续说道：“后来，我成为了一名骑士，一名属于‘守望者’组织的战士女巫。我的训练结合了近身格斗与魔法学习，就像萨瓦兰圣骑士的训练方式一样。我花了大量时间去探索这个世界，保护那些在旅途中执行任务的法师们，有时甚至还会帮助当地的野兽族成员，让他们真正理解‘人类’意味着什么。 啊，那些住在山丘上的可爱小女牛仔……你能看出，我对她们依然有着特殊的感情吧？”她调皮地说着，用手托着那充满奶汁的胸脯。“当一切都崩溃的时候——当我们的古代法师们最后一次犯下不可饶恕的错误时，我试图保护的，正是她们，而不是我自己的同胞。 对他们来说，已经为时太晚了……
        
        发生了什么事？”

卡西拉再次啜饮着她的葡萄酒，金色的眼睛长时间地注视着你。“一些可怕的事情……同时也是一些奇妙的事情。一种升华……那些古老的法师们，我们中最古老的巫师们，终于陷入了绝境，他们不得不诉诸那些被禁止的灵魂魔法，试图逃离玛瑞斯。 在那个美丽而纯净的世界里，他们感到如此痛苦，如此渴望逃离这个乐园，以至于他们不惜将自己的灵魂作为燃料，试图施展出足以突破“世界之幕”的强大法术。终于有一天，所有的首席法师们一起进入了龙之塔，宣称他们已经找到了突破这道屏障的方法。 到了夜晚，整座城市都被恶魔们淹没了——它们像一股由腐败与堕落构成的潮水一样，从那座高塔中涌了出来。如果有一半的低阶法师能够逃脱，我才会感到惊讶；而那些守卫者，几乎没有一个能够幸存下来。
        
        那是大约二十年前的事了。我们城市的公主莱西丝——那个被宠坏的刁钻丫头——在不久之后就成为了恶魔一族的女王，”卡斯冷笑着说道，“我从来都不知道她的父亲，也就是国王，后来发生了什么。 可能是我害死了他。就在第一个转角处，我正在执行巡逻任务，当我看到那些被腐蚀的火焰在燃烧，那些有翅膀的生物在城墙上空飞舞时，我立刻转身逃跑了。我试图召集那些我遇到的“幸存者”，并把他们带到安全的地方。 有一些由神圣结界保护的兽人小教堂愿意收留我们，但我自己却选择了前往西部的那些小城市——那里居住着大多数非人类种族。我召集了一些战士前来帮忙，共同抵抗这场正在摧毁我们世界的灾难。"
        
        卡斯长长地叹了口气，声音里充满了疲惫。“我失败了。我们的抵抗太微弱、太迟了，最终被那股恶魔的洪流彻底吞没。我被俘虏了，遭受了折磨，最终倒下了。”

“哦。

rows.original: "It was my own mother, too. She was always the greatest of the archmages — besides Lethice, anyway — and she used her blood connection to me as the focus for divination magic to track down city after city to destroy. I had no idea I was the cause of the demons' ceaseless advance until I came face to face with the bitch. Too late, then. She broke me herself — introduced me to the idea of a woman with a cock, then to birthing imps; the little snots made themselves self-perpetuating by my body until my soul came tearing free just like Alissa's. At least I lasted a little longer! And rather than slavering over her cock like a lovesick fool, I... I broke her neck the moment she freed me from my restraints. The only human life I've ever taken. That was a quicker death than she deserved." "Oh." "So that's me, then," Kas says evenly, biting her lip when it starts quivering slightly. "That's the story of the great demon queen: a fallen, failed paladin. Lethice scooped me up a while later, put me to work assembling her personal guard. I had nothing better to do. I moved into mother's lab and got to work, learning alchemy and magic and creating my pretty dragon daughters until Lethice's conquest ended, and she... decided to just sit around and bask in the afterglow, I suppose. I was content to be left alone in the Tower of the Griffon until everything came tumbling down again. Couldn't have happened to a nicer queen, so I snuck off with my dragon-girls and some stolen research on portals and decided to try my own veilbreach. That's when I met you. Aside from watching Aileh and the girls growing up, probably the first actually nice thing that's happened to me in two decades." Kas gives you a faltering smile. "Sorry, I don't mean to be a moodkiller. You did ask, though. You deserved to hear the truth. And perhaps now you can see a little more clearly why I'm so very happy to have met you... to find someone who's not a demon that can care for me despite my... my nature." You lean in and give Kasyrra long, loving kiss. She melts around you, wings trembling and soft cock jumping against your thigh. "A demon's not easy to love, [pc.name]. I know that. For both our sakes... I hope when it's all said and done, I'll be worth it."
rows.translation: 那也是我的亲生母亲。她一直都是最强大的大法师之一——至少在莱西丝之外是这样——她利用自己与我之间的血缘联系，作为占卜魔法的媒介，一路追踪着一个个城市进行破坏。直到我与那个女人面对面，我才意识到，正是我导致了恶魔们不断的侵袭。 那就太晚了。是她自己让我明白了这个道理——她让我认识到，有些女人其实拥有“男性特征”，而后来又让我知道了那些小东西是如何通过我的身体来实现自我繁衍的；直到我的灵魂也像艾丽莎的灵魂一样，被迫从我的身体中挣脱出来。 至少我坚持的时间比她预想的要长一些！而且，我没有像个痴情傻瓜一样对她卑躬屈膝、讨好她……而是在她解除对我的束缚的那一刻，就掐断了她的脖子。那是我有生以来夺去的唯一一条人命。这样的死法，对她来说未免太快了。" "哦原来如此，”卡丝平静地说道，当她的嘴唇开始微微颤抖时，她咬了咬嘴唇。“这就是那位伟大的恶魔女王的故事：一个堕落、失败的圣骑士。后来，莱西丝收留了我，让我负责组建她的私人卫队。 我实在没什么别的事情可做，于是就搬到了母亲的实验室里开始工作，学习炼金术和魔法，还培育出了那些漂亮的龙女儿们。直到莱西斯的征服行动结束，然后她……大概只是决定放松下来，享受那些胜利带来的荣耀吧。 我本来很乐意独自待在狮鹫塔里，直到一切都再次陷入混乱。这种事情发生在一位如此善良的女王身上，实在再合适不过了。于是，我带着我的龙女们，还有一些关于传送门的偷来的研究资料，悄悄离开了那里，决定亲自尝试一次“面纱突破”术。 就是在那个时候，我遇到了你。除了看着艾莉和那些女孩们长大之外，这大概也是二十年来发生在我身上第一件真正美好的事情了。 卡斯勉强挤出一丝笑容：“对不起，我并不是想破坏你的心情。不过你确实问了，你也有权知道真相。也许现在你能更清楚地理解，为什么见到你我会如此开心……遇到一个不是恶魔的人，一个能够不顾我的……我的本性，依然关心我的人。 你倾身向前，给了卡西拉一个漫长而充满爱意的吻。她在你怀里仿佛融化了，翅膀微微颤抖，柔软的鸡冠在你大腿上轻轻跳动。 “要爱上一个恶魔，确实并不容易…… [pc.name]. 我明白这一点。为了我们双方的好……我希望到最后，我的付出能够得到回报。

I moved into mother's lab and got to work, learning alchemy and magic and creating my pretty dragon daughters until Lethice's conquest ended, and she... decided to just sit around and bask in the afterglow, I suppose. I was content to be left alone in the Tower of the Griffon until everything came tumbling down again. Couldn't have happened to a nicer queen, so I snuck off with my dragon-girls and some stolen research on portals and decided to try my own veilbreach. That's when I met you. Aside from watching Aileh and the girls growing up, probably the first actually nice thing that's happened to me in two decades." Kas gives you a faltering smile. "Sorry, I don't mean to be a moodkiller. You did ask, though. You deserved to hear the truth. And perhaps now you can see a little more clearly why I'm so very happy to have met you... to find someone who's not a demon that can care for me despite my... my nature." You lean in and give Kasyrra long, loving kiss. She melts around you, wings trembling and soft cock jumping against your thigh. "A demon's not easy to love, [pc.name]. I know that. For both our sakes... I hope when it's all said and done, I'll be worth it." "

rows.original: "Hmm. That's easy enough, I suppose, but I'm not sure how much I want to tell you. Speaking grandly, I want to open another portal to Mareth and bring the rest of my followers and Dragon Generals over.[flags.WC_SAW_DOTTIRS | They didn't all die in the Winter City. The portal was destroyed too early, thankfully for them. ] After that, I'd just as soon send a tremendous magical bomb through and blow that plane to shreds, or seal it off forever at least. But that's, let's call it a stretch goal. My most immediate plans involve that pretty little soul of yours, and a ritual I've been working on since before I even came to Savarra. Call it an obsession of mine. You're the first being between two whole planes I've encountered with a soul so mutagenic and malleable that it might actually work." "And that ritual is?" Kasyrra smirks, [silly |booping your snoot |flicking your nose ]. "Aren't you the nosey one? What I do with my property is my business." You give Kas a look withering enough to make the demon queen sigh. [flags.KAT_KAS |"Ah, fine. Let's put all our cards on the table then. Like I said, your soul has strange properties due to your exposure to raw aetheric energy, the invisible shadowy stuff from between the planes. I'd probably be in the same boat, if I had a soul. I shielded you from the kinetic blast when the portal discharged, but there was nothing I could do about the radiation — not that you have any idea what term even means, do you? Hmm." Kas tuts, tapping her chin. "Suffice to say, you took a blast of magical energy that changed the very nature of your being. It's why eating and drinking random things changes your body, where normally you'd need alchemy or magic to do so. Or the lack of a soul. See, a soul inside your body anchors you to, well, being you. Keeps you roughly the shape and size you ought to be. Normal people can stretch themselves out or tuck in a little, add a tail or a dick, but the essence is the same. You though, there's hardly a limit to what you can be beyond your own self-imposed limitations — how far your mind will let you stretch before you lose your sense of self. Probably why you haven't turned into an amorphous pile of goo yet." "And how is that useful to you?" "I'm getting there!" Kas laughs, "Because you haven't just turned into a pile of slop, that means you must have an incredibly strong sense of self. I think it's strong enough to have embedded directly into your soul. See, normally, when a demon extracts a soul, it comes out as a pink crystal — magical rock candy, really, useful enough for juicing up magic or powering arcane devices but with no trace of the person to whom it once belonged. You could hold up two pieces of Lethicite and never tell if they belonged to me or to Aileh or to little Caitriss. Your soul, though? If I'm right, it would retain a shred of humanity, but it's morphic enough to change to fit a new vessel. It would produce something new and wonderful and very much not like normal Lethicite." "So, what?" you ask, looking Kas over. "You want to turn me into a demon, so you can turn back human?" The demoness raises a hand disarmingly. "No, no. Not quite. Besides, if I turned human again, I'd lose all this wonderful power of mine. Go back to being that weak little thing that [silly |lost to |let her mind be broken by ] imp dick. No, I rejected my humanity a long time ago. But I want more than what being a demon can give me even still. I want the one thing that has always been outside my reach: I want children, [pc.name]. Not disgusting, soulless little imps. No! Real children, wonderful little babies that will look up at me with eyes full of love and wonder, not just bestial lust and greed like an imp! We demons, father or mother, produce nothing but imps; I never had the chance to be a mother to a human. Now I'll settle for a demon, if I must, but I have been tormented for thirty years with the hunger for a child of my own. Even Aileh and her sisters, as much as I love them all, aren't enough. They aren't my blood, just... just lizardfolk babies that Lethice kidnapped and gave to me to experiment on. I crave to have my womb used as a woman's is meant to: to bear her own offspring. I can't rest until I do so... the hunger torments my sleep, drives my every action. My obsession." "How does my soul help with that!?" Kasyrra smiles, trying to look the predatory demon... but you can see right through her. See the glisten in her eyes from tears just held back. "I am going to take your soul, [pc.name], and implant it directly in my womb. I am sure it will work, at least once, to give me a real child... [pc.hasRealCock |To give us a real child. Because of course, I want you to be the [pc.mf |father |sire ] |Of course, I'm not that picky, [pc.name], if you'd rather be the mother. Once you're a demon like me, I can perform the ritual on you, and play the father's part. As long as it's our child, our blood, that's what matters! ]" "That's my dark and devious plan, [pc.name]. I am convinced it will work. At least once, perhaps permanently. So... there you have it. My cards are on the table. What do you think of my hand, [pc.name]?" |"I'm not quite ready to spill the beans on it, my dear. But it will be glorious. Life changing, for both of us. Perhaps in a way you'll even enjoy. We shall see." Maybe. Maybe not. It's your soul to lose, isn't it? ]
rows.translation: 嗯，我想这应该挺简单的，不过我还不确定该告诉你多少事情。说得夸张一点，我想打开通往玛瑞斯的另一扇通道，把我的其他追随者和那些龙将们都带过去。[flags.WC_SAW_DOTTIRS | 他们没有。 所有 在冬之城死去。幸运的是，那道传送门被摧毁得过早了，否则他们就会遭遇不测。 ] 之后，我完全可以直接发射一枚威力巨大的魔法炸弹，把那架飞机炸得粉碎，或者至少将其永远封存起来。不过，这个嘛，我们可以称之为一个更高的目标吧。我目前最紧迫的计划，是与你那小小的灵魂有关，还有那个仪式——其实在我来到萨瓦拉之前，就已经开始研究这个仪式了。 可以这么说，这是一种执念吧。你是我在两个完全不同的世界中遇到的第一个，其灵魂如此具有变异性且可塑性强，以至于真的有可能让这个计划成功。” “那么，那个仪式是什么呢？” 卡西拉露出了微笑，说道： [silly |翘起鼻子 |哼鼻子 ]你不就是那个爱管闲事的人吗？我如何处理自己的财产，那是我的事。” 你用一种极其冷淡的眼神看着卡丝，让这位恶魔女王都不禁叹了口气。 [flags.KAT_KAS |啊，好吧。那我们就把所有的真相都摊开来说吧。正如我所说，由于你接触到了原始的以太能量——那些存在于各个维度之间的无形能量，你的灵魂具有了一些特殊的性质。如果我也拥有灵魂的话，恐怕也会陷入同样的境地。 当传送门打开时，我保护你免受动能冲击波的伤害，但对于辐射，我却无能为力——其实你根本不知道“辐射”这个词到底是什么意思，对吧？嗯…… 卡斯托托着下巴，轻声说道。 可以这么说，你吸收了一股魔力，这种力量彻底改变了你的本质。这就是为什么吃一些平常的东西也会影响你的身体——在正常情况下，你需要炼金术或魔法才能产生这样的效果。当然，也有可能是因为你没有灵魂。 你看，你体内那个灵魂将你与……紧密相连。 做你自己它能让你保持大致应有的体型和大小。普通人可以适当拉伸身体或调整体型，比如加长尾巴或突出某些部位，但本质还是一样的。然而，你认为，只要不自我设限，你的形象其实可以有无限多种可能——你的想象力能让你延伸到什么程度，而不会让你失去自我认知呢？ 大概这就是为什么你还没有变成一堆无定形的黏液吧。” “那这对你来说有什么用呢？” “我马上就明白啦！”卡斯笑着说，“因为你们…… 还没有 它竟然变成了一堆糊状物，这说明你的自我意识一定非常强烈，强到几乎已经融入了你的灵魂之中。你知道，通常情况下，当恶魔提取一个人的灵魂时，提取出来的会是一块粉红色的水晶——其实就是一种魔法糖果，既可以用来增强魔力，也可以为一些神秘装置提供能量，但其中完全不会留下原来拥有这灵魂之人的任何痕迹。 你可以拿起两块莱西蒂特，却永远无法分辨它们是属于我、艾莱，还是小凯特丽丝。但你的灵魂呢？如果我的判断没错，它依然会保留一丝人性，同时也具有足够的可塑性，能够适应新的载体。 它会创造出一些全新而奇妙的东西，这些东西绝对与普通的莱西蒂特不同。” “那么，你想怎么样？”你问道，目光注视着卡丝，“你想把我变成恶魔，这样你就能变回人类了吗？” 女恶魔摊开双手，显得毫无恶意。 不，不。不是这样的。而且，如果我再次变回人类，我就会失去所有这些强大的能力。那样我就又会变回那个弱小无力的家伙了…… [silly |败给…… |让她的精神因此受到打击 ] 该死的。不，很久以前我就抛弃了自己作为人类的身份。但即便如此，我仍然渴望得到超越恶魔身份所能给予我的东西。我想要那件一直遥不可及的东西……我想要它。 孩子们, [pc.name]这些并不是令人作呕、没有灵魂的小怪物。不！它们其实是真正的孩子，是那些会用充满爱与好奇的眼神看着我的可爱婴儿，而不是像那些小怪物一样，只充满兽性的欲望与贪婪！我们这些恶魔，无论是作为父亲还是母亲，生下的都只是这些小怪物；我从来就没有机会成为一个真正的人类母亲。 现在，如果非得这样的话，我也就认命成为恶魔吧。但三十年来，我一直被渴望拥有自己孩子的念头所折磨。即使是我深爱的艾莉和她的姐妹们，也不足以满足我的这份渴望。她们毕竟不是我的亲生骨肉，只是……那些只是莱西丝绑架过来让我用来做实验的蜥人婴儿罢了。我渴望让自己的子宫能够像女人那样发挥作用——生下自己的孩子。直到实现这个愿望，我才能安心……这种渴望折磨着我的睡眠，驱动着我做出每一个行动。 我的 痴迷我的灵魂怎么可能帮上忙呢！？” 卡西拉微笑着，试图给这个凶残的恶魔一种假象……但人们却能看穿她的伪装，看到她眼中那被强忍住的泪水。“我会夺走你的灵魂，”她说道。 [pc.name]然后把它直接植入我的子宫里。我相信这至少会有一次奏效，让我真正拥有一个孩子…… [pc.hasRealCock |为了给予 我们 一个真正的小孩子。因为，我当然希望你能够成为那样的人。 [pc.mf |父亲 |陛下 ] |当然，我并没有那么挑剔。 [pc.name]如果你更愿意扮演母亲的角色的话……一旦你变成了像我这样的恶魔，我就可以为你举行那个仪式，然后扮演父亲的角色。只要是我们自己的孩子，我们的亲生骨肉，这才是最重要的！ ]这就是我的阴险狡诈的计划， [pc.name]我确信这一定会成功。至少会有一次，或许还能永久奏效。所以……我把所有的想法都告诉你了。你觉得我的这个方案怎么样？ [pc.name]?" |亲爱的，我现在还不准备告诉你具体细节。但这件事一定会非常精彩，会改变我们两个人的生活。也许你会觉得其中有些地方甚至会让你感到开心。让我们拭目以待吧。” 也许会，也许不会。毕竟这关系到你的灵魂，不是吗？ ]

rows.original:
"I knew you'd say that!" Kas beams, giving you a kiss on the lips. "I knew you'd understand. My darling [pc.name]!"
            
            Another kiss, and draconic wings are curling around you. "I wish I could make good on that willingness now, but... you're not ready yet. I need you to grow stronger, to face adversity, to nurture that soul of yours until it's as brilliant as a star inside you. Only then can I be one-hundred-percent sure that my ritual will work. So keep fighting. Try and save your world from me, even. I won't blame you — it's all secondary to our real goal. Soon, though, none of it will matter."
            
            The demon queen smiles, forehead pressed to yours in a cocoon of wings. Her fat horsecock twitches against your [pc.thigh]. "Only you and I matter."
            
            "Only you and I."
            
            "So," she murmurs, wings unfurling. "Shall we head upstairs and cement your demonic pact?"
rows.translation:
我就知道你会这么说！”卡丝开心地笑着，然后在你的嘴唇上轻吻了一下。“我就知道你会理解的。我的亲爱的…… [pc.name]又一个吻，紧接着，龙一般的翅膀便环绕在了你的身边。“我真希望能立刻兑现那份承诺，但是……你还没有准备好。我需要你变得更强大，需要你面对种种困境，需要你培养自己的灵魂，让它变得像一颗闪耀的星星一样明亮。 只有这样，我才能百分之百确定我的仪式能够成功。所以，请继续战斗吧。甚至，试着阻止我拯救你们的世界吧。我不会责怪你们的——这一切都只是实现我们真正目标过程中的次要环节。不过，很快，这一切就都不再重要了。恶魔女王微笑着，她的前额紧贴着你的额头，仿佛被翅膀包裹着一样。她那肥大的阴茎在你身上轻轻抽动…… [pc.thigh]只有你和我才重要。”

“只有你和我。”

“那么，”她轻声说道，翅膀缓缓展开，“我们上楼去，把你的恶魔契约正式确定下来吧？

rows.original: Having a child with Kas is worth your soul. You'll be the best — and first — demon parents, together.
rows.translation: 与卡丝生下孩子，绝对值得你付出一切。你们一定会成为最棒、也是第一对恶魔父母。
rows.raw: "Having a child with Kas is worth your soul. You'll be the best — and first — demon parents, together."

rows.original: Kasyrra sets her lips, golden eyes fixating on you. "I understand it's a big ask. You think your soul is something valuable, some intrinsic part of you. But when you lose it, you'll still be you — a more rarified, uninhibited you. And what we'll gain is so much more important! Just... think about it. I would put aside all other goals in pursuit of this. I would do anything for a chance at a true child. So please, my darling, when the time comes... don't hate me when I don't take 'no' for an answer." You grimace. At least she's not trying to take it here and now, but still... "There has to be a better way," you say. "Maybe. But I've yet to discover it. And there's only so long I'm willing to wait... only until that soul of yours shines as brilliant as a star. Until I can be entirely sure that it's strong enough to survive my ritual. If you come up with a better idea before that day comes, I will greet it with open ears and a smile in my heart." Then you have a goal. A hope to keep Kas and your soul. "Now then," Kas says, stroking your cheek. "Enough of such heavy topics for one day, hmm?"
rows.translation: 卡西拉紧闭双唇，金色的眼睛紧紧盯着你。“我明白这是个很高的要求。你认为自己的灵魂是某种珍贵的东西，是你自身不可或缺的一部分。但当你失去它的时候，你依然会…… 你 ——一个更加纯粹、不再受束缚的自我。而我们因此获得的，将会更加重要！ just……好好想想吧。为了实现这个目标，我会放弃所有其他的追求。我愿意这么做。 任何东西 为了能拥有一个属于自己的孩子……所以，请你，亲爱的，当那一刻来临时……如果我选择了拒绝，千万不要恨我。” 你皱了皱眉头。至少她现在并没有强行要求我立刻做出决定，不过还是让人有些困扰。一定有更好的办法，”你说。 “也许吧。但我还没有找到它。而我愿意等待的时间也是有限的……只等到你的那份光芒能够像星星一样闪耀，直到我完全确信它足够强大，能够经受住我的仪式。 如果在那天到来之前你想出了更好的办法，我会敞开双耳、怀以微笑迎接它。” 这样，你就有了一个目标——一个守护卡丝和你的灵魂的愿望。 “那么，”卡丝一边说，一边轻抚着你的脸颊。 一天之内讨论这么严肃的话题已经够多了，嗯？
rows.raw: " Kasyrra sets her lips, golden eyes fixating on you. "I understand it's a big ask. You think your soul is something valuable, some intrinsic part of you. But when you lose it, you'll still be you — a more rarified, uninhibited you. And what we'll gain is so much more important! Just... think about it. I would put aside all other goals in pursuit of this. I would do anything for a chance at a true child. So please, my darling, when the time comes... don't hate me when I don't take 'no' for an answer." You grimace. At least she's not trying to take it here and now, but still... "There has to be a better way," you say. "Maybe. But I've yet to discover it. And there's only so long I'm willing to wait... only until that soul of yours shines as brilliant as a star. Until I can be entirely sure that it's strong enough to survive my ritual. If you come up with a better idea before that day comes, I will greet it with open ears and a smile in my heart." Then you have a goal. A hope to keep Kas and your soul. "Now then," Kas says, stroking your cheek. "Enough of such heavy topics for one day, hmm?" "

rows.original: Having a kid with Kas is great and all, but losing your soul for it? Becoming a demon? It's all a little much...
rows.translation: 和卡斯生孩子固然很棒，但为此失去自己的灵魂？甚至变成一个恶魔？这些未免有些过分了……
rows.raw: "Having a kid with Kas is great and all, but losing your soul for it? Becoming a demon? It's all a little much..."

rows.original: "No?" Kas sneers. You're suddenly aware of her claws on your [pc.skin]; of the heat of her draconic breath across your cheeks. "My dear, don't mistake my friendliness. I'm not asking for your permission. I'm telling you what will happen, when your soul is matured enough. When that little mutation in your chest shines like a star, I will be there to take it. I appreciate our connection, [pc.name], but don't mistake our dalliances for an opportunity to escape your fate. I hope to convince you to choose it willingly. I want you to want it. But if you don't..." Kas lurches to her feet, sudden enough to knock you to the ground[pc.isPregnant | — or would, if she didn't catch you at the last moment, a hand on your pregnant belly ]. "Kas!" you start to say, backing away. "Wait!" She snarls. "Wait for what — you to change your mind? You to show pity for the poor childless demoness? Pft. Go. Get out of here... I don't want to see you for a while. I will... give you time to change your mind." Or change Kas's, perhaps... Kas's mood's turned dour enough you wonder if she'd try to take your soul early just because she has the chance. You peel yourself off the floor and retreat while you can.
rows.translation: 不是吗？”卡斯冷笑道。你突然感觉到了她那些爪子正紧紧抓住你的…… [pc.skin]她那炽热如龙息的气息轻拂着你的脸颊。“亲爱的，不要误解我的善意。我并不是在征求你的同意，而是在告诉你：当你的灵魂足够成熟，当你胸中的那个微小变化像星星一样闪耀时，我会在那里迎接它。我珍视我们之间的这种联系…… [pc.name]但请不要将我们的短暂相处视为逃避命运的机会。我希望能够说服你，让你自愿做出这个选择。我渴望如此。 你 你当然想要它。但如果你不……” 卡丝猛地站了起来，动作如此突然，以至于把你撞倒了。[pc.isPregnant | ——要不是她在最后一刻抓住了你的手，阻止你触碰自己隆起的腹部，你或许就会那么做了。 ]卡斯！”你边说着边开始后退，“等等！” 她怒吼道：“等什么？等你改变主意吗？等你表现出诚意来吗？ 真可怜 那个没有孩子的可怜女恶魔？哼，快滚吧……我暂时不想见到你。我会给你时间……让你改变主意的。 或者，也许能让Kas改变主意吧…… Kas现在的情绪已经糟糕到了极点，你甚至怀疑她会不会趁现在这个机会就夺走你的灵魂。你赶紧从地上爬起来，趁还来得及赶紧离开。
rows.raw: " "No?" Kas sneers. You're suddenly aware of her claws on your [pc.skin]; of the heat of her draconic breath across your cheeks. "My dear, don't mistake my friendliness. I'm not asking for your permission. I'm telling you what will happen, when your soul is matured enough. When that little mutation in your chest shines like a star, I will be there to take it. I appreciate our connection, [pc.name], but don't mistake our dalliances for an opportunity to escape your fate. I hope to convince you to choose it willingly. I want you to want it. But if you don't..." Kas lurches to her feet, sudden enough to knock you to the ground[pc.isPregnant | — or would, if she didn't catch you at the last moment, a hand on your pregnant belly ]. "Kas!" you start to say, backing away. "Wait!" She snarls. "Wait for what — you to change your mind? You to show pity for the poor childless demoness? Pft. Go. Get out of here... I don't want to see you for a while. I will... give you time to change your mind." Or change Kas's, perhaps... Kas's mood's turned dour enough you wonder if she'd try to take your soul early just because she has the chance. You peel yourself off the floor and retreat while you can. "

rows.original: You'll never surrender your soul to Kasyrra, no matter how much you may enjoy your lurid relationship.
rows.translation: 无论你多么享受与卡西拉的这段关系，你也永远不会将自己的灵魂献给她。
rows.raw: "You'll never surrender your soul to Kasyrra, no matter how much you may enjoy your lurid relationship."

rows.original: "You... you what?" Kas balks, looking rapidly between your face and chest. "No, I can still sense it, I can damn well smell the soul on you! What do you mean you don't have it anymore — you're no demon[pc.ra demonmorph |, no matter how you look on the outside ]!" "I'm not," you say, "but I did give my soul to one of the Seven. It's just a placeholder you're sensing, something to fill the void." The demon queen's eyes harden, and her hand plants itself open-palmed on your chest. "How... but I can't sense... it's even still growing! Marae's tits, it's just a fucking reflection, isn't it? How could I be so dense!? How could I have thought you..." Kas snarls, standing up so suddenly that you're nearly thrown from the couch. "Damn you, [pc.name]. Damn you to the deepest depths of the void. Your soul was my only chance... the first real chance I've ever had, you damned fool, and you threw it away for what!?[pc.ra kitsune | For fluffy fucking tails!? ]" "So what if I did?" you shoot back. "[pc.ra kitsune |You jealous? You wanna touch them? ]It's mine to give away, not yours to take." An open palm crosses your cheek hard enough to make the couch rock under you, nearly spilling you over the back. By the time the stars have cleared from your vision, Kasyrra's winged back to the fireplace and has her hand on her axe, stopping just short of lifting it. She's faced away from you, face bathed in shadows. "Get out," she snarls. "For the sake of whatever love I bore you: get the fuck out before I cleave that worthless head from your body! If I have to make war on the very gods of this realm to achieve my goals, then so be it. I will sunder the heavens and corrupt your damned Seven, just as Lethice did to the old gods of Mareth. I will not be denied!" "Kas, wait!" She heaves up the runic axe onto her shoulder, turning towards you with a fiendish fire burning in her eyes. "I cared about you; I would have made you the [pc.mf |king |queen ] to rule at my side if you asked, and all I wanted... I told you from the start what I wanted in return. Yet you squandered it on a whim, for a being that will never care about you half as deeply as I do... as I did. No more. Go now, before my anger consumes me entirely. Go, while I still hold out some hope that the pale reflection in your chest might somehow be of use to me, someday. That your false soul will someday lead me to the real thing, wherever it may be." No matter how strong you've grown, fighting the demon queen in her deepest lair is a losing proposition. You beat a retreat from the manor as quick as you can, wincing when the doors slam heavily behind you — followed by a howl of rage and desolate loss that promises to haunt your dreams for the rest of your life. A life that might well be cut short the next time you see this queen of demons.
rows.translation: 你……你什么？”卡斯结结巴巴地问道，目光在你的脸和胸口之间快速移动。“不，我仍然能感觉到，我确实能闻到你身上散发出的那种气息！你说你不再拥有它了，是什么意思——你根本就不是恶魔啊！[pc.ra demonmorph |无论你外表如何 ]我不是，”你说道，“但我确实将自己的灵魂献给了七人之一。你所感受到的，只不过是一个替代品，用来填补那空虚罢了。” 恶魔女王的目光变得冰冷，她的手掌掌心朝上，放在了你的胸前。 怎么……但我感觉不到……它还在继续变大啊！玛拉的胸部，那只不过是该死的反射而已，对吧？我怎么会这么蠢呢！我怎么会以为你……” 卡斯怒吼着，突然站了起来，吓得你差点从沙发上摔下来。 该死的， [pc.name]去你的吧，堕入无尽的虚空吧。你的灵魂曾是我唯一的希望……那是我有生以来第一次真正拥有的机会，你这个该死的傻瓜，却为了什么把它给毁了！？[pc.ra kitsune | 就为了那些毛茸茸的尾巴啊！？ ]那如果我这么做了又怎样？”你反问道。[pc.ra kitsune |你吃醋了？你想碰它们吗？ ]这是我可以送给别人的东西，而不是你能够夺走的东西。” 一只张开的手掌狠狠地拍在你的脸上，力度之大使得你坐在沙发上身体都微微摇晃，差点从沙发背上摔下来。等到你的视线重新清晰起来时，卡西拉已经站在壁炉旁，手中握着她的斧头，却只是停在举起的动作上，没有继续动手。 她背对着你，脸淹没在阴影中。“滚出去，”她厉声说道，“看在我曾经对你怀有的一丝感情的分上：赶紧滚开，否则我会把你那毫无价值的头颅从你身上砍下来！如果为了达到我的目的，我甚至不得不与这个世界的神明为敌，那也只好如此了。 我会撕裂苍穹，摧毁你们那些该死的七神，就如同莱西丝当年对马雷斯的古老神明所做的那样。我一定会做到。 不是 被拒绝了！” “卡丝，等等！” 她将那把符文斧扛在肩上，转身朝你走来，眼中燃烧着炽热的怒火。“我曾经关心过你；我本来是想让你成为…… [pc.mf |国王 |女王 ] 如果你愿意，我可以陪在你身边共同掌管一切。而我所想要的……从一开始我就告诉过你，我期望得到什么作为回报。然而你却随心所欲地将其浪费掉了，送给一个永远不可能像我这样深爱着你的人……至少我曾经是那样爱你的。现在，不再有了。 现在就走，趁我的愤怒还没有完全吞噬我。走吧，只要我心中还抱有一丝希望——希望你胸腔中那微弱的闪光有朝一日能对我有所帮助；希望你那虚假的灵魂能引领我找到真正的东西，无论它藏在何处。无论你变得多么强大，去恶魔女王的最隐秘巢穴与她战斗都注定是失败。你以最快的速度从那座庄园中逃了出来，当大门在身后重重关上的那一刻，你不禁皱起了眉头——紧接着，那恶魔女王发出的愤怒与绝望的嚎叫，将会伴随你余生，成为你梦中的噩梦。 下次你见到这位恶魔女王时，你的生命很可能就会终结。

A life that might well be cut short the next time you see this queen of demons. '

rows.original: Kas's plan has something of a wrinkle in it: you don't actually have your soul anymore. She's going to have a hard time taking it from your patron deity.
rows.translation: 卡斯的计划其实存在一个漏洞：你实际上已经不再拥有自己的灵魂了。她要想从你的守护神那里夺走这份灵魂，将会面临很大的困难。
rows.raw: "Kas's plan has something of a wrinkle in it: you don't actually have your soul anymore. She's going to have a hard time taking it from your patron deity."

rows.original: "That's.... a difficult question," Kas says, flapping her smaller pair of wings. "What makes a demon tick? I suppose you've already seen the way in which we're 'born' — that each of us was once a mortal who was separated from her soul. For most, that separation occurs after a great deal of hedonistic sex — even if unwanted, so much pleasure simply seems to overwhelm the soul and cause it to crystalize. We call the stone that leaves the body Lethicite, after the first Queen of Demons back in my native plane. Once your soul is gone, your body's free to change however you want, whenever you want. With just a thought, I could transform myself into a strapping ten-foot-tall man with six dicks, or a petite little thing with no curves at all. Perhaps even mimic my human body, for a time, but we all have a form that comes naturally to us. An expression of who we are, deep down inside — what you see before you. It's almost embarrassing how Alissa just copied me!" Kasyrra gives a hearty laugh, rolling her eyes. "Ah well, she was already half a demon by the time I found her." "What do you mean, exactly?" "A demon is... a creature of single-minded obsession. Even me. We all embody some deep-seated desire that consumes us inside and out. Back in Mareth, I remember some scholars tried to typify demons into specific emotions: lust, wrath, envy... but that's too simplistic. We're all sinful creatures, all lustful even if that lust isn't carnal. And there are demons that don't think entirely about sex. Just not many, considering most are made into demons by having a whole lot of sex until their brains break. Now, Alissa... she was driven entirely by her obsessions before I even met her. Caged up and tortured her little sister, for what? Why, to have a child with her. A pureblooded heir to the Ridell line. Her inner desires were entirely focused on the Duchess of the Rim; when her soul crystalized, that drive to dominate the younger princess became all-consuming, it became her everything. For most demons it's at least a little surprising to find themselves wholly consumed by their darkest desires!" "So," you ask, "is that all a demon is? An obsessed shapeshifter?" Kas shrugs. "If you wish to break us down to the very simplest expression, yes, that's accurate. Of course we're powerful, not limited by a soul — a demon warrior can turn herself into a hulking brute of pure muscle, and my magic is far superior to what any but the mightiest of ancient magisters could ever hope to achieve. I will admit, though, there are downsides to demonhood — chief of which is the inability to sire or birth trueborn children. That right is taken from us forever when we become demons. Instead, all we can give rise to are wretched little imps, beings of unrestrained, animalistic desire, worthless and incapable of love. But perhaps there will soon be a way to overcome that, and if so, then the tradeoff will finally be made worthwhile. It's quite remarkable, really, what even the weakest of true demons can achieve compared to their mortal selves. And the strongest of us? I could easily crush a whole company of warrior-mages as skilled as I was when I was at the height of my human life!"
rows.translation: 这……是个很难回答的问题，”卡丝说道，她那对较小的翅膀轻轻扇动着。“究竟是什么驱使着恶魔行动呢？我想你们应该已经看到了我们‘诞生’的过程——我们每一个恶魔，原本都是凡人，只是他们的灵魂被分离了出来罢了。 对大多数人来说，这种分离发生在经历了大量充满享乐性质的性行为之后——即使这些行为并非出于自愿，但如此多的快感似乎会压倒人的灵魂，使其发生某种“结晶”般的转变。我们将这种从人体中分离出来的“物质”称为“莱西石”，这个名字来源于我故乡位面第一位恶魔女王。 一旦你的灵魂离开身体，你的躯体就可以随心所欲地发生变化，随时都可以改变形态。只要我有一个念头，就能把自己变成一个身高十英尺、拥有六根阴茎的壮汉，或者变成一个毫无曲线、身材娇小的小东西。 或许甚至会在一段时间内模仿我的人体形态，但每个人都有与生俱来的外形——那是我们内心深处的自我表达，也是你们眼前所看到的样子。艾丽莎竟然完全模仿了我，这简直有点尴尬吧！" 卡西拉开怀大笑，翻了个白眼：“唉，当我遇到她的时候，她已经有一半是恶魔了。” “你到底是什么意思？” “恶魔啊……就是那种被某种执念完全支配的生物。就连我也是如此。我们每个人内心都怀有某种根深蒂固的欲望，这种欲望会彻底吞噬我们。在马雷斯的时候，我记得有些学者试图将恶魔归纳为特定的情绪：欲望、愤怒、嫉妒……但这种分类太过简单化了。我们所有人都是有罪的生物，都充满了欲望，哪怕这种欲望并非肉体上的。而她…… 是 那些并不完全被性所驱使的恶魔……其实这样的恶魔并不多，因为大多数恶魔都是因为在过度沉溺于性行为中，导致大脑受损才变成恶魔的。现在，艾莉莎……在我遇见她之前，她就已经完全被自己的执念所控制了。 把她关起来折磨她的妹妹，究竟是为了什么？只是为了和她生一个孩子——一个纯血统的里德尔家族继承人罢了。她内心的渴望始终只指向那位边缘公爵夫人；当她的灵魂结晶之后，那种想要支配这位年轻公主的欲望便变得无比强烈，彻底占据了她的心灵。 一切对大多数恶魔来说，发现自己完全被自己最黑暗的欲望所控制，这种感觉至少会让他们感到有些惊讶吧！” “那么，”你问道，“恶魔就只是这样的存在吗？只是些被欲望所控制的变形者而已吗？” 卡什耸了耸肩：“如果你想用最简单的词语来描述我们的话，那么没错，这种说法确实很准确。 我们当然拥有强大的力量，不会受到灵魂的限制——恶魔战士可以将自己变成一只由纯粹肌肉构成的庞然怪物，而我的魔法也远超除了那些最强大的古代法师之外的任何人的能力范围。 不过我必须承认，成为恶魔也有其不利之处——其中最严重的一点就是无法生育真正的后代。当我们变成恶魔的那一刻，这个权利就永远被剥夺了。我们所能诞生的，只有那些卑劣的小恶魔，它们充满了无节制的兽性欲望，毫无价值，也根本不懂什么是爱。 但或许不久之后就会出现某种方法来克服这一难题，如果真的如此，那么这种权衡最终就会变得值得。事实上，即便是最弱小的真正恶魔，与它们 Mortal 时的状态相比，所展现出的能力也确实令人惊叹。 而我们当中最强的人呢？在我人生巅峰时期，我的实力足以轻易击败一整个由技艺高超的战士法师组成的团队！

Instead, all we can give rise to are wretched little imps, beings of unrestrained, animalistic desire, worthless and incapable of love. But perhaps there will soon be a way to overcome that, and if so, then the tradeoff will finally be made worthwhile. It's quite remarkable, really, what even the weakest of true demons can achieve compared to their mortal selves. And the strongest of us? I could easily crush a whole company of warrior-mages as skilled as I was when I was at the height of my human life!" '

rows.original: "My [flags.WC_SAW_DOTTIRS |poor babies," Kas laments. |darling daughters!" Kasyrra says proudly. ] "At least, they are as close to children as I am capable of at present. The Dragon Generals were a project given to me by Lethice: she wicked witch stole a number of lizan — what you in this world call pexighast — children from a village after her soldiers conquered it. She wanted me to turn them into the ultimate corrupted soldiers to marshal her forces and lead them to victory in some great future war she envisioned. I used a mix of alchemy and magic to change them, make them smarter and stronger and more majestic than anything they could have ever hoped to be as mere lizans! I made them glorious!" You grimace. "They were children." "What? Do you think throwing them to a bunch of succubi would have been better; or should Lethice have just butchered them? I kept them safe from the war, the tide of demons washing over the land. I trained them to fight, sculpted them into goddess of war! And where Lethice wanted them to fight each other until only four survived to become her generals, I kept every single one of them alive and cared for in secret! I adore[flags.WC_SAW_DOTTIRS|d] each and every one of them. They [flags.WC_SAW_DOTTIRS|were|are] like children to me. The closest I have to the real thing. I did as well by them as I could." [aileh.friendLover |"You did a good job with Aileh, at least," you admit. The huffy dragoness has very little ill to speak of her 'mother' despite it all. |"You did an amazing job with Aileh," you admit. She is amazing, isn't she? |"So about Aileh..." ] "Ah, Aileh," Kas says, smiling warmly. "The eldest of the bunch, and the one I'm proudest of. Just don't let her hear that. Hmm, she's got an ego, that one. Can't imagine where she got it!" With a wink, Kas continues, "[aileh.friendLover |I'm glad the two of you have gotten along so well. It fills me with joy to see her adapting to this new world! |Yes, I know you two have gotten quite close, haven't you? That's good. She deserves someone like you. |I hope you two get the chance to know each other before long. ] She was always the strongest and most commanding of the girls. Would have made an incredible general, if Lethice had ever followed through with her war plans. The most willful of them, too, after that rascal Kiha." "Kiha?" "Ah, another of the Dragon Generals, the youngest of the bunch. Always had to play the rebel; never could wrap her head around the favor I'd done her and the others. Thought I was the worst monster of the lot, though I suspect if she'd ever come to the queen's court she'd have changed her tune in a hurry. [flags.WC_SAW_DOTTIRS |I suppose she was the winner in the end, though. |It's a shame, too, I think she'd have really liked this world quite a bit more. ] Kiha escaped from the Tower of the Dragon a few months before I made my exit from Mareth, went to live in the swamplands like some common spiderling. Hmph! Last I heard, though, she'd shacked up with that Ingnamite crusader that was about to kick Lethice's door down. With any luck she got to meet the queen after all — and gave Lethice my deepest apologies for leaving her high and dry before lopping off her head!"
rows.translation: 我的 [flags.WC_SAW_DOTTIRS |这些可怜的孩子们，”卡斯叹息道。 |亲爱的女儿们！”卡西拉自豪地说道。 ] 至少，他们目前还算是最接近孩子的存在了。这些‘龙将’其实是莱西丝交给我的任务：那个邪恶的女巫在她的军队占领了一个村庄后，抢走了那里的许多孩子——在你们这个世界，这些孩子被称为‘佩克希加斯特’。 她希望我将他们变成终极版的堕落战士，这样她就能集结自己的军队，在她所设想的某场伟大的未来战争中带领他们取得胜利。我运用炼金术与魔法的结合，改变了他们的形态，使他们变得比作为普通的利赞人时所能想象到的任何东西都要聪明、强大、威武。 是我做的。 太棒了！你皱起了眉头：“他们毕竟还是孩子啊。” “什么？你难道觉得把他们扔给一群女妖会更好吗？还是说让莱西丝直接把他们全部杀掉才对？我保护他们免受战争的伤害，免受恶魔入侵的威胁。我训练他们战斗，把他们培养成战争女神！而当莱西丝想让他们互相残杀，最终只留下四个人来成为她的将军时，我却偷偷地保护着他们每一个人，悉心照料他们！我真的很喜欢他们……[flags.WC_SAW_DOTTIRS|d] 他们中的每一个人。他们所有人。 [flags.WC_SAW_DOTTIRS|是|是] 对我来说，它们就像孩子们一样。它们是我与真实世界最为接近的东西。我尽自己所能去照顾它们。 [aileh.friendLover |至少你在照顾艾蕾这件事上做得还不错，”你承认道。尽管发生了那么多事，这位脾气暴躁的龙女对她的‘母亲’其实并没有太多负面的评价。 |你在处理Aileh的事情上做得非常出色，”你由衷地称赞道。她…… 是 太神奇了，不是吗？ |那么，关于艾莉…… ] 啊，艾莉赫，”卡斯微笑着说道，“她是这群人里最年长的，也是我最引以为傲的人。只是别让她听到我这么说哦。嗯，她可是个很有自尊心的人呢，真不知道这种自尊心是从哪里来的！” 卡斯眨了眨眼，继续说道：[aileh.friendLover |我很高兴你们俩相处得这么融洽。看到她逐渐适应这个新环境，我真的感到非常开心！ |是的，我知道你们两个关系变得相当亲密，对吧？那很好。她确实值得拥有像你这样的人。 |希望你们俩能尽快有机会互相了解。 ] 在所有女孩中，她一直都是最坚强、最有领导力的人。如果莱西丝当初能够将她的军事计划付诸实施，她一定会成为一名出色的将军。而且，在那些女孩中，她也是最固执的——除了那个调皮捣蛋的基哈之外。啊，又是那位龙将之一，这群人中最年轻的那位。她总是扮演反叛者的角色，从来都不理解我为她和其他人所做的那些事。她认为我是这群人中最恶劣的家伙，不过我怀疑，如果她有机会来到女王的宫廷，她的看法很快就会改变的。 [flags.WC_SAW_DOTTIRS |不过，我想她最终还是成为了赢家。 |这也真是太遗憾了，我觉得她肯定会非常喜欢这个世界的。 ] 在我离开玛瑞斯之前的几个月，基哈从龙之塔逃了出来，然后像一只普通的蜘蛛幼崽一样，去了沼泽地生活。哼！不过据我所知，她后来和那个即将冲进莱西丝家的英格纳米特十字军战士同居了。但愿她最终还是见到了女王——并且替我向莱西丝致以最深的歉意，因为我当时在砍下她的头之前，竟然把她丢在了那里，让她陷入了绝境！

rows.original: Kas answers with a shrug, reaching for her wine. When she speaks after a long sip, her tone's shifted from your milfy lover to that of a teacher. "I really don't know. Nobody does. We call them souls, but they act more like... like bundle of latent energy. They aren't unique to any one person once they're outside of the body, anyway: they all crystalize into Lethicite, and become all but indistinguishable from one another. In that form, they're useful enough as a power source, but have no real spiritual significance." "I know it's some form of self-control mechanism when it's still inside the body. It keeps you from changing your bodyshape too radically, and limits your magical potential. It also seems to be emotional self control. I can tell you with certainty I'd never have done half the things I do when I was human; couldn't have dreamed of the depravity. The same goes for most of the demons I knew back home: sweet sisters of the Marethian church became the most sadistic rapists; good-hearted knights of the Watch became bloodthristy reavers. Desires once held close to the chest become overriding obsessions, sometimes to the exclusion of all else. Consider yourself lucky that you fell for a demoness with remarkable discipline! Lesser fiends would just lock you up in the basement and have their way with you for eternity..." There's your familiar succubus's lilt. Kas laughs heartily, grabbing your ass and stealing a kiss. "Oh, yes, I've had nearly two decades to relearn how to control myself. Even I was just a monster when it started, driven to breed like a base beast in heat... and there's plenty of people who'd say I've not changed a bit, I'm sure! Any being that lacks a soul simply loses all the restraint, the empathy, the... well, their humanity, love. We're something else entirely. Something free." "Free? What do you mean free?" Kas gives you a particularly smug look. "Free of our soul's bindings. I can be anything I want to. I am unlimited and unrestrained. There is no limit to what a demon can achieve. Even... even the impossible, with enough patience and determination. And I have plenty of both to spare." [pc.isSoulbound |Considering your current predicament, you feel compelled to point out, "Not everything that loses its soul becomes a demon, Kas. Look at [pc.ra kitsune |valkyries |kitsunes ]." "Yes, I've.... I've heard of a few races that this world's gods have touched. According to Tollus — if you care to trust that toad — such creatures' souls are held in trust by their patron gods. Some sort of communal soul they are all connected to. It's like a halfway point between us and you. An interesting point, though, and one I've asked Tollus to look into for me. Apparently one of his favored pets is some sort of soulbound assassin. It's strange, I never sensed anything off about her when we met... and apparently she's quite capable of having children, too, if only of her own race. This soul-sorcery bears close examination, when next the opportunity presents itself. Thank you for the reminder." | "That being said," Kas says, "I've heard that this world's gods favor certain races. Allow them to exist in some sort of half-souled state, bound up by some communal vessel full of soul-stuff rather than having one of their own. I've asked Tollus to look into it for me. Apparently one of his favored pets is some sort of soulbound assassin. It's strange, I never sensed anything off about her when we met... and apparently she's quite capable of having children, too, if only of her own race. Can the gods truly perform such a feat of soul-sorcery? It's hard to believe, but I suppose it bears remembering that there's always something in the world more powerful than even I am. For now, at least." ]
rows.translation: 卡丝耸了耸肩，然后拿起酒杯。喝了一口酒之后，她的语气从之前那种性感迷人的样子变成了老师的口吻：“我真的不知道。没人知道。我们把它们叫做‘灵魂’，但它们的行为却更像是…………就像一团蕴含着巨大能量的物质。一旦脱离人体，这些能量并不会只属于某一个人；它们都会结晶成“莱西石”，从而变得彼此之间几乎没有区别。在这种状态下，它们确实可以作为一种能源使用，但并不具备任何真正的精神意义。我知道，当这种力量还存在于体内时，它其实是一种自我控制的机制。它能防止你的体型发生过于剧烈的变化，同时也会限制你的魔法潜力。看起来，它还有其他一些作用…… 情感化的 自我控制力……我可以肯定地告诉你，如果我还是人类的话，绝对做不出我现在所做的这些事；那些堕落的行为，我当时根本想象都想不到。我家乡遇到的那些恶魔们也是如此：原本是玛瑞西亚教会里善良的修女，后来却变成了最残忍的强奸犯；原本是守卫军团中心地善良的骑士，后来却变成了嗜血的掠夺者。 那些曾经被紧紧珍藏在心间的渴望，往往会变成令人无法抗拒的执念，有时甚至会让人忽略其他一切。庆幸吧，你竟然爱上了这样一个拥有严苛自律的恶魔女！要是换作那些低等的邪魔，他们早就把你关在地下室里，然后永远占有你了吧……就是这种你熟悉的声音啊……”卡丝开心地大笑起来，一把抓住你的屁股，偷了一个吻。“哦，没错，我花了将近二十年的时间才重新学会控制自己。刚开始的时候，我也只不过是一头野兽罢了，会像发情的野兽一样疯狂地繁殖。而且，肯定有很多人会说，我一点都没有变啊！任何没有灵魂的存在，都会彻底失去所有的克制力、同理心……嗯，也就是他们会失去所有那些应该拥有的东西。 人类爱，我们是截然不同的存在。某些东西…… 免费免费？你是什么意思？ 免费卡斯露出一种极其得意的表情：“摆脱了我们灵魂的束缚，我现在可以成为任何我想成为的人。我不再受到任何限制，也没有任何束缚。对于一个恶魔来说，没有什么是不可能的。只要拥有足够的耐心和决心，就连不可能的事情也能实现。而我，这两样东西都绰绰有余。 [pc.isSoulbound |考虑到你目前的处境，你觉得有必要指出：“并不是所有失去了灵魂的东西都会变成恶魔，卡斯。看看吧…… [pc.ra kitsune |女武神 |狐狸精 ]是的，我……我听说过这个世界上有些种族受到了神明的眷顾。据Tollus所说——如果你愿意相信那只蟾蜍的话——这些生物的灵魂被他们的守护神所庇护。可以说，这些灵魂是它们与守护神之间的一种共同联系。 它就像是我们与你之间的一个中间点。不过，这确实是个有趣的现象，我也让托卢斯去调查了一下。据说他最喜欢的宠物之一就是某种与灵魂相连的刺客。奇怪的是，当我们见面时，我并没有感觉到她有什么特别之处。……显然，她也应该完全有能力生孩子，只不过只能生自己种族的孩子而已。下次有机会的时候，一定要仔细研究这个现象。谢谢你的提醒。 | 话虽如此，”卡斯说道，“我听说这个世界上的神明偏爱某些种族。它们被允许以一种半灵魂的状态存在，这些灵魂被束缚在某种共同的容器之中，而不是拥有属于自己的灵魂。 我已经让托卢斯帮忙调查这件事。据说他最喜欢的宠物中有一只属于那种与灵魂紧密相连的刺客类型。真是奇怪，我们见面时我完全没有察觉到她有什么特别之处……而且据说她也是能够生育后代的，只不过只能生育同种族的幼崽而已。 诸神真的能够施展如此强大的灵魂魔法吗？这实在难以置信，但我想，我们应该记住：这个世界上总是存在着某些比我更强大的存在。至少目前是这样。 ]

rows.original: "Believe it or not," Kas says, "I found it in a dream. The night I first came to this world, amid that terrible storm, I flew north and spent the night saving that pretty little flower in the forest — you know the one. [flags.ALRAUNE_KILLED|The one you murdered. Ass.|Intimately.] I drifted off to sleep in her arms in the frigid cold, and woke up... just outside the gates, here." You squint. "You just... fell into the Ways Between in your sleep?" "It happens." She shrugs, sipping her wine. "To me, at least, ever since I've arrived in Savarra. Pretty often, too. So, I awoke at the gates and let myself in. Thought it was a fever dream, or that I'd died and gone to some afterlife. I can't say who might have built this place. I brought one of my new succubi around after we met in the Winter City, a scholar, and she babbled on about some ancient emperor or another from some place called Belhar and how it was the most amazing discovery in her life and oh how very wet she'd become. Ha! I gather that at some point in the distant past this empire was able to navigate the Ways more easily than anyone today and just... sent workers and building material up here to construct the palace. Why or how, I don't know." Right, of course Kas doesn't really have any context for Savarran history, does she. "It seems this was some archmage's dreambound retreat," Kasyrra continues. "And considering the heart-shaped orgy bed and half the sexy toys in the dungeon were here before I was, I can only imagine it was a very fun place to be. Can't imagine why they'd have let it fall to the wayside. Hmm, Wayside, get it?" Ha. "Did your scholar have any idea who the mage might have been?" She shakes her head. "We're right above a small tower, but I have a feeling a mage that built such a meager holding as that in the real world wouldn't be responsible for this. Perhaps the tower was a doorway in at one point. There's apparently a large fortress buried under the mountains nearby; perhaps that place's owner might have been this one's too. Whoever they might have been, they're clearly long gone now." [var0 |Actually now that she mentions it, you're pretty sure you saw the Gunvaldsen arms on the landing upstairs. The Jarl's wife was a witch of some repute, wasn't she? Maybe Jarlessa Thyri had a hand in building this place. [var1 |You remember something about Thyri having been deeply interested in studying Dreamwalkers — creatures like the Lureling — and speculating that the Ways and Dreams were connected somehow. If Kas really just woke up in the Ways, is it possible she's a Dreamwalker too? Her homeworld must not have any equivalent metaphysical structure, so perhaps coming here awoke some latent power. Hard to say for sure; as much as Kas seems to trust you, you doubt she'd submit for a detailed experimental analysis on her psionic abilities. ] ] Kas shrugs, pouring herself another glass. "Regardless of who and why, it's mine now. And what's mine is yours, my dear. Enjoy the place to your heart's content."
rows.translation: 信不信由你，”卡斯说道，“我是在梦中找到它的。就在我第一次来到这个世界的那个夜晚，在那场可怕的暴风雨中，我飞向北方，花了一整晚的时间去拯救森林里那朵漂亮的小花——你应该知道的那一朵。 [flags.ALRAUNE_KILLED|就是你杀害的那个混蛋。|亲密地。] 在刺骨的寒风中，我在她怀里渐渐睡着了，醒来时……已经来到了这里，就在城门的外面。 你眯起眼睛问道：“你是说……在睡梦中就进入了那些通道？” “这种事确实会发生。”她耸了耸肩，继续喝着酒。 至少对我来说，自从我来到萨瓦拉之后，这种情况发生得相当频繁。有一次，我在城门边醒来，然后自己走了进去。我当时以为那只是个发烧引起的幻觉，或者是我已经死了，来到了某个来世。我也说不上来是谁建造了这个地方。 在冬季之城见面后，我带了一位新认识的女子同行——她是一位学者。她滔滔不绝地谈论着某个叫做贝尔哈尔的地方的一位古代皇帝，还说这是她一生中最重要的发现，还提到自己当时兴奋得简直无法自控。 哈！看来在遥远的过去，这个帝国能够比今天的任何人都要轻松地利用那些通道，于是他们就把工人和建筑材料运到这里来建造这座宫殿。具体为什么、又是如何做到的，我就不知道了。没错，卡丝当然对萨瓦兰的历史一无所知，对吧？” “看来这里曾经是某位大法师用来冥想、追求梦想的地方，”卡西拉继续说道，“而且考虑到那个心形的群交床，还有地牢里那些半数以上的性感玩具，在我来到这里之前就已经存在了，我只能推测这里曾经是…… 非常 一个很有趣的地方。真不明白他们为什么会让它逐渐被忽视、荒废掉。嗯…… 道路边儿，明白了吗？” 哈。“你的学者有没有猜到那个法师可能是谁？” 她摇了摇头。“我们正位于一座小塔的正上方，但我有一种感觉，在现实世界中，一个会建造如此简陋据点的法师，不可能会负责这件事。也许这座塔曾经是某种通道的入口……显然，在附近的山脉下面埋着一座巨大的堡垒；也许…… 那个 这个地方的主人可能也曾是这个人的所有者。无论他们当时是谁，显然现在已经不在人世了。 [var0 |其实现在她这么一提，你确实觉得自己在楼上楼梯间看到过Gunvaldsen家族的纹章。那位男爵的妻子可是一位颇有名气的女巫，对吧？也许Jarllessa Thyri也在建造这个地方的过程中发挥了作用。 [var1 |你应该还记得，Thyri曾经对研究那些被称为“梦行者”的生物——比如Lureling这样的存在——表现出极大的兴趣，她还推测，“道路”与“梦境”之间必定存在着某种联系。如果Kas真的只是…… 醒来 按照这些规律来看，她会不会也是个“梦行者”呢？她的家乡肯定没有类似的形而上学结构，所以也许来到这里之后，她体内某些潜在的能力被唤醒了。不过这也很难说准；虽然卡丝看起来很信任你，但你怀疑她是否会愿意让自己接受关于自己灵能能力的详细实验分析。 ] ] 卡丝耸了耸肩，又给自己倒了一杯酒。“无论这是谁的，也不管出于什么原因，现在这一切都是我的了。而属于我的东西，自然也属于你，亲爱的。请尽情享受这个地方吧。
rows.raw: " "Believe it or not," Kas says, "I found it in a dream. The night I first came to this world, amid that terrible storm, I flew north and spent the night saving that pretty little flower in the forest — you know the one. [flags.ALRAUNE_KILLED|The one you murdered. Ass.|Intimately.] I drifted off to sleep in her arms in the frigid cold, and woke up... just outside the gates, here." You squint. "You just... fell into the Ways Between in your sleep?" "It happens." She shrugs, sipping her wine. "To me, at least, ever since I've arrived in Savarra. Pretty often, too. So, I awoke at the gates and let myself in. Thought it was a fever dream, or that I'd died and gone to some afterlife. I can't say who might have built this place. I brought one of my new succubi around after we met in the Winter City, a scholar, and she babbled on about some ancient emperor or another from some place called Belhar and how it was the most amazing discovery in her life and oh how very wet she'd become. Ha! I gather that at some point in the distant past this empire was able to navigate the Ways more easily than anyone today and just... sent workers and building material up here to construct the palace. Why or how, I don't know." Right, of course Kas doesn't really have any context for Savarran history, does she. "It seems this was some archmage's dreambound retreat," Kasyrra continues. "And considering the heart-shaped orgy bed and half the sexy toys in the dungeon were here before I was, I can only imagine it was a very fun place to be. Can't imagine why they'd have let it fall to the wayside. Hmm, Wayside, get it?" Ha. "Did your scholar have any idea who the mage might have been?" She shakes her head. "We're right above a small tower, but I have a feeling a mage that built such a meager holding as that in the real world wouldn't be responsible for this. Perhaps the tower was a doorway in at one point. There's apparently a large fortress buried under the mountains nearby; perhaps that place's owner might have been this one's too. Whoever they might have been, they're clearly long gone now." [var0 |Actually now that she mentions it, you're pretty sure you saw the Gunvaldsen arms on the landing upstairs. The Jarl's wife was a witch of some repute, wasn't she? Maybe Jarlessa Thyri had a hand in building this place. [var1 |You remember something about Thyri having been deeply interested in studying Dreamwalkers — creatures like the Lureling — and speculating that the Ways and Dreams were connected somehow. If Kas really just woke up in the Ways, is it possible she's a Dreamwalker too? Her homeworld must not have any equivalent metaphysical structure, so perhaps coming here awoke some latent power. Hard to say for sure; as much as Kas seems to trust you, you doubt she'd submit for a detailed experimental analysis on her psionic abilities. ] ] Kas shrugs, pouring herself another glass. "Regardless of who and why, it's mine now. And what's mine is yours, my dear. Enjoy the place to your heart's content." "

rows.original: "Hmm? You heard that godling, didn't you? I was told very explicitly that yes, it's very possible to restore a soul to a demon... for a god, anyway. Such soul-sorcery is far beyond anything any demon or mortal is capable of. Believe me, some of us have tried for far, far too long. There was even a process this one demon fellow, an archmage I used to know, worked out a few years back: he tried using lightning strikes, back when Mareth still had lightning, to try and fuse souls back into their host bodies. Just ended up turning the Lethicite this incandescent blue color, and frying the unlucky demon half to death. Only about half of us survived, and none of us better off for it." "Us?" Kas's lips draw taut a moment, eyes looking elsewhere in the room. "I.... yes, there were a number of demons involved in the trial. Held in secret out in the wastelands, in this old library he'd commandeered. Burnt down just before I left, though most of his research survived him." You lean in, almost close enough to bump noses with Kas. "What do you mean us?" "Shit," the demon says, scowling. "What? You're not stupid, [pc.name], what do you want me to say?" "I want you to tell me you tried to get your soul back." Kasyrra's lips stay drawn in a hard line for a moment, and she refuses to make eye contact. If she wasn't sitting down, you're sure her tail'd curl under her leg like a struck puppy. When she finally speaks, it's barely more than a whisper. "I... let an insane demon wizard nearly kill me... trying to put my soul back in. Happy?" "Not that you almost died," you tell her, taking the pouting demon's cheeks and bringing her in for a kiss. She returns it limply, clearly upset. Instead, Kas runs a hand up your chest and under your [pc.armor], flicking her fingers around the waystone charm she gave you when this all began, that glistening blue gemstone. "I kept it all this time," she whispers. "Some vague hope that somehow, somewhat, I'd find a way to restore what was taken from me. Instead, I met you... I met you, and I knew my twisted old soul was worthless in comparison to yours." The demoness catches an emotional second wind, enough to grab you under the ass and flip the pair of you around on the couch so that she's straddling you now, soft cock resting on your stomach and breasts all but blurring your vision. "You're a diamond, [pc.name]. No, more than that. You're everything. The key to it all. A soul so bright it's blinding... and to think, it's inside a person so radiant that such a soul pales in comparison." She kisses you, tentative at first, as if seeking your assent. When you return it, hands wandering up her broad flanks, she pushes it: her tongue slips between your [pc.lipsChaste], hands running all over your [pc.chest]. Your head sinks back into the cushions under the vigor of her passionate assault, drowning in forked demonic tongue and the heavy, fluid-full weight of her tits crushing you down. "K-Kas!" you manage to blurt out, before her coal-hued lips lock with yours, silencing you in her tender embrace. When Kasyrra finally peels herself off you, her golden eyes are misty. "Don't make this old succubus say it, [pc.name]. Tell me... tell me that..." [pc.dcb |"Just spit it out," you say, thumbing Kasyrra's chin. "Tell me you love me." "I... I love you, [pc.name]. I love you dearly." |"I love you," you say, caressing Kas's cheek. "You too," she murmurs, taking your hand and kissing it. "I love you so very, very much." |"Awww, I love you too, Kassy," you giggle, leaning up and giving her another kiss. "Y-yes, that's it," Kasyrra sighs. "Love..." ] She leans back on her bestial haunches, smiling down at you and wiping at an eye. "How terribly unbecoming of me. I can hardly-" "[pc.isBimbo |But I can! |Oh, shut up, ]" you say, grabbing Kas's shoulders and pulling her back down into another kiss. All the haughty demonic might bleeds out of Kasyrra as you wrap her up in your arms, kissing her just as passionately as you would any mortal lover. Her thick cock throbs and trembles, pressed between your bodies. Who could have thought a demon queen and a mortal champion could fall in love, and yet here you are, cradling this wicked being in your arms and locking lips despite the tears now streaming down her cheeks. Tears of joy. Tears brought on by being loved in turn. Oh, Kas... Perhaps there's some hope that, somehow, you can reverse what's been done to her. [pc.isDK |Or at least find some way of unlocking her power for yourself without the sacrifices that were forced upon her. |If there's some way to reverse demonhood, you'll just have to find it! ]
rows.translation: 嗯？你听到那个小神灵说的话了吧？有人明确告诉我，确实有可能将灵魂重新注入恶魔体内……至少对神明来说是这样。这种灵魂操控的技艺，远远超出了任何恶魔或凡人的能力范围。 相信我，我们中的一些人已经尝试了太久太久了。几年前，我曾经认识的一位大法师想出了一个方法：他尝试利用闪电——在那时，玛瑞斯还拥有闪电之力——来将灵魂重新融合到宿主体内。 结果我们把那种紫晶石染成了炽热的蓝色，还把那个倒霉的恶魔烧成了半死不活的状态。最终只有我们中的一半人活了下来，而且没有人因此而得到任何好处。” “我们？” 卡斯的嘴唇紧绷了一下，目光转向房间的另一处。 我……是的，审判过程中确实涉及了许多恶魔。审判是在荒野中的那座被他占用的旧图书馆里秘密进行的。在我离开之前，那座图书馆被烧毁了，不过他的大部分研究资料都保存了下来。 你凑近了一些，几乎要和卡斯鼻子碰在一起了。“你是什么意思？ 我们该死，”恶魔皱着眉头说道，“什么？你又不傻啊…… [pc.name]你到底想让我说什么？” “我想让你承认，你确实尝试过找回自己的灵魂。” 卡西拉的嘴唇紧抿成一条僵硬的线，她拒绝与对方进行眼神交流。如果她不是坐着的话，她的尾巴肯定会像受惊的小狗一样蜷缩在她的腿边。 当她终于开口说话时，声音几乎只是轻声细语：“我……让一个疯狂的恶魔巫师差点害了我一命……只是为了把我的灵魂重新放回身体里。这样你满意了吗？” “并不是因为你差点丧命，”你告诉她，同时捏了捏那个撅着嘴的恶魔的小脸，把她拉过来吻了她一下。 她无力地把它还了回去，显然非常沮丧。 然而，卡斯却将手伸到你的胸前，然后从你的……下面穿过。 [pc.armor]她轻轻拨动着那颗在你一切遭遇开始时她送给你的路标宝石——那颗闪闪发光的蓝色宝石。“我一直保存着它，”她轻声说道，“怀着一线渺茫的希望，希望有一天能找到办法，把从我这里夺走的东西夺回来。 相反，是我遇见了你……当我遇见你时，我意识到，与我相比，我那扭曲而苍老的灵魂简直一文不值。 这个女恶魔重新找回了力量，她用力将你拽到沙发上，让自己骑坐在你身上，她的阴茎紧贴着你的腹部和胸部，几乎让你的视线都变得模糊不清。 你真是一颗钻石， [pc.name]不，远不止如此。你就是一切。是这一切的关键。你的灵魂如此明亮，简直让人睁不开眼……想想看，这样一个光彩照人的人，拥有这样一颗璀璨的灵魂。” 她吻了你，起初有些犹豫，仿佛在等待你的回应。当你回应她的吻时，你的手开始沿着她宽厚的身躯缓缓向上移动，她便进一步主动起来：她的舌头滑入了你的口中…… [pc.lipsChaste]双手在你的身上游走…… [pc.chest]在她热情而激烈的攻势下，你的头被迫深深地埋进枕头中，那分叉的恶魔之舌不断侵扰着你，而她那饱满、沉重的胸部更是将你压得喘不过气来。“卡……卡西！”你终于挤出这几个字，但她的煤黑色双唇立刻紧紧地封住了你的嘴唇，让你在她的温柔怀抱中彻底失去了声音。 当卡西拉终于从你身上离开时，她那金色的双眼已经充满了泪水。“别让这个老女妖说出这句话来…… [pc.name]告诉我……告诉我那个…… [pc.dcb |干脆说出来吧，”你说道，用拇指轻轻捏了捏卡西拉的下巴，“告诉我你爱我。” “我……我爱你。 [pc.name]我非常爱你。 |我爱你，”你轻抚着卡斯的脸颊说道。 “我也爱你，”她轻声回答，握住你的手并吻了吻它。“我真的非常非常爱你。 |哎呀，我也爱你，卡西，”你笑着说，身体前倾，又给了她一个吻。 “是……是的，就是这样，”卡西拉叹了口气，“爱情…… ] 她靠在那些粗壮的四肢上，微笑着看着你，同时用手揉了揉眼睛。“我真是太不得体了……我简直……”[pc.isBimbo |但我可以！ |哦，闭嘴吧。 ]你说得对，”他抓住卡丝的肩膀，将她拉回自己怀中，再次吻了她。当你将她紧紧拥入怀中，用同样热烈的情感吻她时，卡西拉身上所有那傲慢的恶魔气息似乎都消失了——你就如同在吻任何一个平凡的恋人一样。 她那粗壮的阴茎在你们的身体之间跳动着、颤抖着。谁能想到，一位恶魔女王与一位凡人勇士会坠入爱河呢？而如今，你们就这样相拥着，尽管她的脸颊上正流淌着泪水，你们依然紧紧地吻在一起。 喜悦的泪水。因为被他人所爱而流下的泪水。 哦，卡丝…… 或许还有一线希望，你能想办法扭转发生在她身上的一切。 [pc.isDK |或者，至少找到某种方法，让自己能够释放她的力量，而无需让她承受那些被迫承受的牺牲。 |如果有什么办法可以解除恶魔的状态，你只需要找到它就行了！ ]

rows.original:
"Hmm? No, why would I?" Kas laughs, tracing a finger down your chest. "Theoretically there's a disconnect between a person and their soul; once it becomes lethicite, it's no longer identifiably yours, just a crystal of condensed power. Of course, it can vary with the majesty of its source, but Alissa's was... it was a tiny, warped thing. Fit her, really. She was a demon long before I arrived — had to distract herself with a cavalcade of hedonism and bloodsports to suppress her obsession for accosting her sister. I just gave her a little push over the edge."
    
    The demoness shrugs. "After we left the city, I flew to the winter queen's gauche new court and returned her soulstone. Who knows what she's done with it since? Could be the gem set in her buttplug now for all I know."
    
    "And where might this court be?" you ask. 
    
    "Tsk tsk. That's not my secret to reveal, my dear. Not that I much care what becomes of her, she's no longer of particular use to me. Still, I'm sure you'll find a clue before long."
rows.translation:
嗯？不，我为什么要那么做呢？”卡斯笑着，用手指轻轻划过你的胸膛，“从理论上来说，一个人与他们的灵魂是分离的；一旦灵魂变成了莱瑟石，它就不再属于你个人了，只不过是一块凝聚了能量的晶体罢了。 当然，这种东西会因其来源的不同而有所差异，但艾莉莎的那个……其实只是个微小而扭曲的东西。真的，它完全适合她。在我来到这里之前，她就已经是个恶魔了——她不得不通过一系列纵情享乐和血腥的活动来转移自己的注意力，才能抑制那种不断骚扰她妹妹的冲动。 我只是稍微推了她一下而已。”

恶魔女耸了耸肩：“我们离开城市后，我飞到了冬之女王那新建立的宫廷，把她的灵魂宝石还给了她。谁知道从那以后她把那宝石用来做了什么？说不定现在那宝石已经被镶嵌在她的肛门装饰物里了呢。那么，这个法庭会设在哪里呢？”你问道。

“啧啧，这可不是我应该透露的秘密，亲爱的。虽然我其实并不太在意她的命运如何，因为她已经不再对我有太大用了。不过，我相信你很快就会找到线索的。"
